record.text: Underwood's distinction feels right. Most people don't mind AI helping with technical work. The discomfort starts when AI begins speaking on someone's behalf. That's where trust gets complicated. Does that line hold as the tools improve?

The agency crisis Warzel describes didn't start with generative AI. Algorithmic feeds were already shaping what we saw, read and discovered long before chatbots arrived. AI just takes it a step further by replacing the human voice on the other end. Is this a point of no return or just another shift?

Trump announced as headliner for U.S. 250th anniversary fair amid artist withdrawals

🤖 IA: It's not clickbait ✅
👥 Users: It's not clickbait ✅

#donaldtrump #anniversarycelebration #uspolitics 

View full AI summary:

external.description:
Former U.S. President Donald Trump is set to play a central role in an upcoming celebration marking America’s 250th anniversary, after several scheduled performers withdrew from the event. The large-scale festival, branded “The Great American State Fair,” will take place on Washington’s National Mall and is organised by Freedom 250, a group that has described itself as non-partisan but was founded with Trump’s involvement and is led by a former State Department official from his administration.

According to organisers, Trump will personally kick off the celebration on June 24, ahead of the broader festivities scheduled from June 25 to July 10. The event is expected to include musical performances, cultural exhibits, family-friendly attractions, and aerial flyovers.

The announcement follows reports that several artists, including Bret Michaels, The Commodores, and Martina McBride, withdrew from performing. Some cited concerns about the event’s political associations or said they had been misled about its non-partisan framing. McBride publicly stated on social media that she believed she had been invited to a neutral celebration but later reconsidered after learning more about its context.

Despite the cancellations, organisers say other performers such as Flo Rida, Fab Morvan of Milli Vanilli, and Vanilla Ice are still expected to participate. Trump commented on social media that artists were “getting the yips” and suggested he would step in as a featured presence, describing himself in highly favourable terms.

The White House has not clarified details regarding scheduling discrepancies in Trump’s statements about the event. The celebration remains positioned as a major national milestone commemorating 250 years of American independence, though it has drawn attention for the intersection of entertainment, politics, and public perception surrounding its organisation.
external.title: Trump announced as headliner for U.S. 250th anniversary fair amid artist withdrawals

RCMP investigates shooting of a foal along McLean Creek Trail in Kananaskis

🤖 IA: It's not clickbait ✅
👥 Users: It's not clickbait ✅

#wildhorses #rcmp #alberta 

View full AI summary:

external.description: The RCMP is investigating the shooting of a foal along McLean Creek Trail, southwest of Bragg Creek, in Kananaskis Country. The foal, believed to be only a few days old, was found injured on May 23 and subsequently euthanized by Kananaskis Emergency Services. The incident occurred within a firearm use restriction zone, which prohibits shooting within one kilometre of the road. Alberta authorities classify free-roaming horses as feral animals under the Stray Animals Act, rather than wildlife, meaning they are not protected by the Wildlife Act. The provincial government has expressed concern over 'unacceptable' wild horse population levels, while advocates for the animals dispute these claims. Wildlife photographer Patti Miyazaki, who has been observing this herd in the Elbow River area, expressed deep distress over the foal's death, highlighting the emotional connection between local observers and the horses. Alberta Fish and Wildlife Enforcement Services did not participate in the investigation, as it involves livestock, leaving the RCMP to handle the case. This incident has sparked outrage among animal advocacy groups and social media communities following Alberta’s wild horse population, raising broader concerns about the management and protection of feral horses in the province.
external.title: RCMP investigates shooting of a foal along McLean Creek Trail in Kananaskis

Finland eliminates Canada 4-2 to reach hockey world championship final against Switzerland

🤖 IA: It's not clickbait ✅
👥 Users: It's not clickbait ✅

#icehockey #worldchampionship #finlandcanada 

View full AI summary:

external.description:
Finland secured a 4-2 comeback victory over Canada in the ice hockey world championship semi-final, booking its place in the final against Switzerland. The result ends Canada’s hopes of winning the tournament for a second consecutive year and forces the team into a bronze-medal match against Norway. Canada had entered the tournament as a favourite, led by high-profile players such as Macklin Celebrini and Sidney Crosby, and had been dominant in group play, including a win over the United States in the quarter-finals. However, the Canadians have now suffered consecutive semi-final disappointment following last year’s upset loss to Denmark.

Canada initially took a 2-1 lead in the first period with goals from Robert Thomas and Dylan Holloway, while Finland responded through Patrik Puistola. The momentum shifted dramatically in the second period when Finland scored three unanswered goals. Aleksander Barkov tied the game early in the period, followed by Konsta Helenius and Aatu Räty, who struck 1:22 apart to give Finland a 4-2 lead. Mikael Granlund contributed two assists, and goaltender Justus Annunen made 28 saves to help preserve the lead.

Canada’s Jet Greaves made 17 saves but could not stop Finland’s surge, particularly during a dominant second period in which Finland outshot Canada 10-3. Despite late pressure and pulling the goalie, Canada could not close the gap. In the other semi-final, Switzerland defeated Norway 6-0, earning a place in the final for the third consecutive year. Leonardo Genoni recorded a 20-save shutout, while Switzerland continues its pursuit of a first-ever world championship title. The final is scheduled for Sunday, with Finland aiming for its fifth championship and first since 2022.
external.title: Finland eliminates Canada 4-2 to reach hockey world championship final against Switzerland

Aleksander Barkov tied the game early in the period, followed by Konsta Helenius and Aatu Räty, who struck 1:22 apart to give Finland a 4-2 lead. Mikael Granlund contributed two assists, and goaltender Justus Annunen made 28 saves to help preserve the lead.

Canada’s Jet Greaves made 17 saves but could not stop Finland’s surge, particularly during a dominant second period in which Finland outshot Canada 10-3. Despite late pressure and pulling the goalie, Canada could not close the gap. In the other semi-final, Switzerland defeated Norway 6-0, earning a place in the final for the third consecutive year. Leonardo Genoni recorded a 20-save shutout, while Switzerland continues its pursuit of a first-ever world championship title. The final is scheduled for Sunday, with Finland aiming for its fifth championship and first since 2022.
external.title: Finland eliminates Canada 4-2 to reach hockey world championship final against Switzerland

🤖 IA: It's clickbait ⚠️
👥 Users: It's clickbait ⚠️

View full AI summary:

external.description:
A public dispute emerged between Washington Post Pentagon reporter Dan Lamothe and Sean Parnell, Assistant Secretary of the Army for Manpower and Reserve Affairs, over reporting related to UFC ticket distribution involving U.S. troops. The controversy centers on claims that service members were being encouraged to attend a UFC event associated with the White House, with some reporting suggesting troops were asked to cover their own travel expenses and that attendance was limited by certain eligibility criteria.

Parnell criticized the original reporting, describing it as misleading and arguing that the White House and UFC were instead offering a valuable opportunity for service members and their families to attend a unique, once-in-a-lifetime experience. He framed the initiative as generous and beneficial to military personnel, rejecting the negative characterization implied in the story.

Reporter Dan Lamothe responded publicly on social media, stating that Parnell’s team had declined or failed to provide comment for a full day before publication and did not engage even after the article was released. He defended the accuracy of the reporting, reiterating that the UFC has long been popular among troops, that there was an organized effort to identify interested service members, and that certain logistical conditions applied, including requirements related to travel costs and physical eligibility standards.

Lamothe emphasized that all reported facts were verified and stood by the story, concluding his remarks with a dismissive sign-off. The exchange highlights ongoing tensions between political officials and journalists, particularly regarding narratives that emerge after officials choose not to respond during the reporting process and later publicly dispute published accounts.
external.title: Reporter and Pentagon correspondent dispute Trump official’s claims over UFC ticket outreach to U.S. troops

必死に生き抜いたあなたを、夜が優しく包んでくれることを祈って…。

      * How do LLMs handle code tokens differently from text tokens?



## What Is an LLM Token? Unpacking the Building Blocks of AI Text

A **_token_** in the context of Large Language Models (**LLMs**) is the **_fundamental unit_** of text that the model processes, influencing everything from text generation speed to accuracy. Understanding what an LLM token is allows us to better grasp how these complex AI systems work.

### The Tokenization Process: Breaking Down Language

Large Language Models (LLMs) don’t understand language in the same way humans do. Instead, they process text by breaking it down into smaller, discrete units called _tokens_. This process, known as _tokenization_ , is crucial for enabling LLMs to understand, generate, and manipulate human language. **What is an LLM token?** It’s a question of how we convert human-readable text into a format computers can process.

### Different Tokenization Methods

Different LLMs employ varying tokenization methods. Some common approaches include:

  * **Word-based tokenization:** This simple method splits text based on spaces. However, it can lead to a large vocabulary size and challenges in handling rare words.

  * **Character-based tokenization:** This method treats each character as a token. While it can handle rare words effectively, it struggles to capture the relationships between characters.

  * **Subword tokenization:** This approach strikes a balance between word-based and character-based methods. It breaks words into smaller, more frequent subwords, such as prefixes, suffixes, or stems. Common subword tokenization algorithms include:

    * _Byte Pair Encoding (BPE)_ : Merges the most frequent character pairs iteratively.
    * _WordPiece_ : A variant of BPE used by BERT, focusing on maximizing likelihood.
    * _Unigram Language Model_ : Uses a probabilistic model to determine subword units.



Subword tokenization is the most widely used method in modern LLMs because it effectively handles rare words and maintains a manageable vocabulary size.

### Impact of Tokenization on LLM Performance

The choice of tokenization method can significantly affect an LLM’s performance. A well-chosen tokenizer can lead to:

  * **Improved Efficiency** : Smaller vocabulary sizes often translate to faster processing and reduced memory requirements.

  * **Better Generalization** : Subword tokenization helps the model handle unseen words by breaking them into familiar components.

  * **Increased Accuracy** : Effective tokenization enables the model to capture semantic relationships more accurately.




However, a poorly chosen tokenizer can result in:

  * **Reduced Performance** : Inefficient tokenization can hinder the model’s ability to understand and generate text.

  * **Increased Resource Consumption** : Large vocabulary sizes can lead to higher memory usage and slower processing.




### Token Limits and Context Windows

LLMs have limitations on the number of tokens they can process in a single input or output. This constraint, known as the _context window_ or _token limit_ , determines the maximum length of text the model can effectively handle. Longer context windows allow models to process more complex and nuanced information, but they also require more computational resources. Exceeding the token limit can lead to truncation or errors.

### Tokenization Examples

Here are some examples of how different tokenizers might handle the sentence “I am walking quickly to the store”:

Tokenizer Type | Tokens
---|---
Word-based | `I`, `am`, `walking`, `quickly`, `to`, `the`, `store`
Character-based | `I`, ``, `a`, `m`, ``, `w`, `a`, `l`, `k`, `i`, `n`, `g`, ``, `q`, `u`, `i`, `c`, `k`, `l`, `y`, ``, `t`, `o`, ``, `t`, `h`, `e`, ``, `s`, `t`, `o`, `r`, `e`
Subword (e.g., BPE) | `I`, `am`, `walking`, `quick`, `ly`, `to`, `the`, `store`

Notice how the subword tokenizer breaks “quickly” into “quick” and “ly,” representing these common components as separate tokens. **What is an LLM token?** In this example, it is a blend of whole words and partial words to optimize representation and processing.

### Common Mistakes Understanding Tokens

A common misconception is that a token is equivalent to a word. While this can be true in some cases, especially with word-based tokenization, it’s not always accurate. Subword tokenization often breaks words into smaller units, meaning a single word can be represented by multiple tokens. Another common mistake is assuming all tokens are created equal. Some tokens, especially those representing rare words or specific characters, might have a disproportionate impact on the model’s performance. Finally, ignoring the _context window_ limitations can lead to unexpected outputs or errors.

### Tools for Tokenization and Counting

Various tools and libraries are available for tokenizing text and counting tokens, including:

  * **Hugging Face Transformers Library:** A popular Python library that provides pre-trained tokenizers for various LLMs.
  * **spaCy:** A Python library for advanced Natural Language Processing (NLP) tasks, including tokenization.
  * **Online Token Counters:** Many websites offer free online tools for counting tokens in a text string using different tokenization schemes.



By using these tools, developers can better understand the tokenization process and optimize their models for performance.

### Frequently Asked Questions (FAQs)

#### **What is the typical token length for an LLM?**

Token length varies depending on the LLM and the tokenization method used. Generally, tokens are short, often consisting of a few characters or a partial word. The average length tends to be around _4 characters per token_ , but this is just a rule of thumb.

#### **How does tokenization affect the cost of using an LLM?**

Many LLM providers charge based on the number of tokens processed. Both input and output tokens contribute to the cost. Therefore, understanding tokenization is _crucial for cost optimization_. Longer inputs will cost more, and generating longer responses will also increase expenses.

#### **Can I customize the tokenization process for an LLM?**

While you can’t usually change the core tokenizer of a pre-trained LLM, you can sometimes influence how it’s used. Fine-tuning on a specific dataset can subtly shift the token representation and the model’s understanding of _token relationships_.

#### **What’s the difference between a token and an embedding?**

A _token_ is the raw unit of text. An _embedding_ is a vector representation of that token, capturing its semantic meaning within the model’s learned space. Embeddings are the numerical representations that the model uses for computation.

#### **How do LLMs handle emojis and special characters?**

Most modern LLMs are equipped to handle emojis and special characters, often tokenizing them as individual tokens or subword units. _The effectiveness depends on the training data and the specific tokenizer used_.

#### **What happens if I exceed the token limit?**

If you exceed the token limit, the LLM typically truncates the input or output, leading to potentially incomplete or inaccurate results. Some advanced models employ techniques like _attention mechanisms_ to manage longer contexts better, but they still have limits.

#### **Is there a standard tokenization method for all LLMs?**

No, there is no single _standard tokenization method_. Different LLMs use different techniques, and the choice of tokenizer depends on factors such as the model architecture, the training data, and the desired performance characteristics.

#### **How can I determine the token count of a text for a specific LLM?**

The best way to determine the token count for a specific LLM is to use the _tokenizer provided by the LLM’s API or SDK_. Many LLM providers offer tools for tokenizing text and counting tokens before submitting a request.

#### **Why is tokenization important for machine translation?**

Tokenization plays a _vital role_ in machine translation by breaking down sentences into manageable units that the model can process and translate. It helps the model align words and phrases between languages and generate accurate translations.

#### **What are the challenges of tokenizing languages like Chinese or Japanese?**

Languages like Chinese and Japanese, which don’t use spaces to separate words, pose unique challenges for tokenization. LLMs often employ _more sophisticated tokenization techniques_ such as character-based or subword-based tokenization to handle these languages effectively.

#### **Can adding more tokens always improve the quality of the generated text?**

Not necessarily. While providing more context can often improve the quality of generated text, there’s a point of diminishing returns. _Exceeding the optimal context window or providing irrelevant information_ can sometimes degrade performance.

#### **How do LLMs handle code tokens differently from text tokens?**

Some LLMs, especially those trained on code, may use _specialized tokenizers_ for code that recognize programming language keywords, operators, and other code-specific elements. This allows the model to better understand and generate code. Other models treat code as another form of text.

Longer context windows allow models to process more complex and nuanced information, but they also require more computational resources. Exceeding the token limit can lead to truncation or errors.

### Tokenization Examples

Here are some examples of how different tokenizers might handle the sentence “I am walking quickly to the store”:

Tokenizer Type | Tokens
---|---
Word-based | `I`, `am`, `walking`, `quickly`, `to`, `the`, `store`
Character-based | `I`, ``, `a`, `m`, ``, `w`, `a`, `l`, `k`, `i`, `n`, `g`, ``, `q`, `u`, `i`, `c`, `k`, `l`, `y`, ``, `t`, `o`, ``, `t`, `h`, `e`, ``, `s`, `t`, `o`, `r`, `e`
Subword (e.g., BPE) | `I`, `am`, `walking`, `quick`, `ly`, `to`, `the`, `store`

Notice how the subword tokenizer breaks “quickly” into “quick” and “ly,” representing these common components as separate tokens. **What is an LLM token?** In this example, it is a blend of whole words and partial words to optimize representation and processing.

### Common Mistakes Understanding Tokens

A common misconception is that a token is equivalent to a word. While this can be true in some cases, especially with word-based tokenization, it’s not always accurate. Subword tokenization often breaks words into smaller units, meaning a single word can be represented by multiple tokens. Another common mistake is assuming all tokens are created equal. Some tokens, especially those representing rare words or specific characters, might have a disproportionate impact on the model’s performance. Finally, ignoring the _context window_ limitations can lead to unexpected outputs or errors.

### Tools for Tokenization and Counting

Various tools and libraries are available for tokenizing text and counting tokens, including:

  * **Hugging Face Transformers Library:** A popular Python library that provides pre-trained tokenizers for various LLMs.
  * **spaCy:** A Python library for advanced Natural Language Processing (NLP) tasks, including tokenization.
  * **Online Token Counters:** Many websites offer free online tools for counting tokens in a text string using different tokenization schemes.



By using these tools, developers can better understand the tokenization process and optimize their models for performance.

### Frequently Asked Questions (FAQs)

#### **What is the typical token length for an LLM?**

Token length varies depending on the LLM and the tokenization method used. Generally, tokens are short, often consisting of a few characters or a partial word. The average length tends to be around _4 characters per token_ , but this is just a rule of thumb.

#### **How does tokenization affect the cost of using an LLM?**

Many LLM providers charge based on the number of tokens processed. Both input and output tokens contribute to the cost. Therefore, understanding tokenization is _crucial for cost optimization_. Longer inputs will cost more, and generating longer responses will also increase expenses.

#### **Can I customize the tokenization process for an LLM?**

While you can’t usually change the core tokenizer of a pre-trained LLM, you can sometimes influence how it’s used. Fine-tuning on a specific dataset can subtly shift the token representation and the model’s understanding of _token relationships_.

#### **What’s the difference between a token and an embedding?**

A _token_ is the raw unit of text. An _embedding_ is a vector representation of that token, capturing its semantic meaning within the model’s learned space. Embeddings are the numerical representations that the model uses for computation.

#### **How do LLMs handle emojis and special characters?**

Most modern LLMs are equipped to handle emojis and special characters, often tokenizing them as individual tokens or subword units. _The effectiveness depends on the training data and the specific tokenizer used_.

#### **What happens if I exceed the token limit?**

If you exceed the token limit, the LLM typically truncates the input or output, leading to potentially incomplete or inaccurate results. Some advanced models employ techniques like _attention mechanisms_ to manage longer contexts better, but they still have limits.

#### **Is there a standard tokenization method for all LLMs?**

No, there is no single _standard tokenization method_. Different LLMs use different techniques, and the choice of tokenizer depends on factors such as the model architecture, the training data, and the desired performance characteristics.

#### **How can I determine the token count of a text for a specific LLM?**

The best way to determine the token count for a specific LLM is to use the _tokenizer provided by the LLM’s API or SDK_. Many LLM providers offer tools for tokenizing text and counting tokens before submitting a request.

#### **Why is tokenization important for machine translation?**

Tokenization plays a _vital role_ in machine translation by breaking down sentences into manageable units that the model can process and translate. It helps the model align words and phrases between languages and generate accurate translations.

#### **What are the challenges of tokenizing languages like Chinese or Japanese?**

Languages like Chinese and Japanese, which don’t use spaces to separate words, pose unique challenges for tokenization. LLMs often employ _more sophisticated tokenization techniques_ such as character-based or subword-based tokenization to handle these languages effectively.

#### **Can adding more tokens always improve the quality of the generated text?**

Not necessarily. While providing more context can often improve the quality of generated text, there’s a point of diminishing returns. _Exceeding the optimal context window or providing irrelevant information_ can sometimes degrade performance.

#### **How do LLMs handle code tokens differently from text tokens?**

Some LLMs, especially those trained on code, may use _specialized tokenizers_ for code that recognize programming language keywords, operators, and other code-specific elements. This allows the model to better understand and generate code. Other models treat code as another form of text.

»Caroline Ramsey« von Rina Hidaka
Die Heldin des Otome-Games »Caroline and the Rainbow Magic Sweets«. Wie im Spiel sucht sie Kontakt zu den fünf Love Interests und wird dadurch zu einer Bedrohung für Pia und die anderen Verlobten. Auf den ersten Blick scheint sie die Eroberungsrouten einfach nur zu genießen, doch dahinter verbirgt sich offenbar ein Geheimnis.
»Erin White« von Sora Amamiya
Eine Nebenfiguren-Villainess aus dem Otome-Game, die genau wie Pia verurteilt werden soll. Sie ist Henrys Verlobte und vom Charakter her das genaue Gegenteil der schüchternen Pia: hell, lebhaft und aktiv. Als Henry sich gemäß der Spielhandlung zur Heldin hingezogen fühlt, wird Erin schwer verletzt. Pia steht ihr dabei als Freundin zur Seite.
»Henry Cox« von Haruki Ishiya
Der Sohn des Ritterordensleiters und Erins Verlobter. Er ist einer der Love Interests des Otome-Games. Henry ist ehrlich, fröhlich und geradlinig, ohne versteckte Absichten. Weil er zu allen freundlich ist, fühlt er sich wie im Spielszenario zur Heldin hingezogen und verletzt dadurch Erin.

»»Even Though I’m a Super Timid Noble Girl, I Accepted the Bet From My Cunning Fiancé« entsteht bei Jumondo (Villainess Level 99) unter der Regie von Nobuaki Nakanishi (I Shall Survive Using Potions!, Don’t Hurt Me, My Healer!). Die Serienkomposition übernimmt Yuko Fukuda, während das Charakterdesign von Hyun-sik Choi stammt. Die Soundregie liegt bei Jin Aketagawa und die Musik komponieren Moe Hyuga und Natsuki Hamada, während KADOKAWA für die Musikproduktion verantwortlich ist.
